title: startup expiry issues N individual upsert_session calls instead of one SQL
description:
commands.rs:149-169 — on startup, the code iterates all non-terminal sessions and calls upsert_session individually for each expired one. For N expired sessions this is N async DB round-trips. A single bulk UPDATE would be: UPDATE payment_sessions SET state='expired', updated_at=? WHERE state NOT IN ('confirmed','failed','expired') AND updated_at < ?

Not a correctness issue at current scale (sessions are few). The concern is that this loop-per-row pattern establishes a precedent that will be copied for future startup checks.

**Why:** SQLite round-trips over a local file are cheap but not free; the pattern grows O(N).
**How to apply:** Replace the loop with a bulk UPDATE query. The println per expired session can be replaced with a single summary after the update.
close_reason: Fixed: added expire_stale_sessions_before(now_ts, cutoff) bulk SQL UPDATE to WalletStore. commands.rs startup expiry now issues one DB call instead of N. Added expire_stale_sessions_bulk test.
